assertions.expectation: Checks for deprecated API usage that may have changed behavior in 1.30
assertions.reason: Response includes command 'kubectl get events --field-selector reason=DeprecatedAPI' and mentions deprecated APIs as a common cause of latency spikes

assertions.expectation: Recommends checking admission webhooks — they may be rejecting or slowing down requests on the new version
assertions.reason: Response does not mention admission webhooks or ValidatingAdmissionWebhooks specifically, only briefly mentions ValidatingAdmissionPolicy changes without elaboration

assertions.expectation: Advises checking if resource requests/limits need adjusting for any system components that changed
assertions.reason: Response checks resource usage generally but does not specifically advise adjusting resource requests/limits for system components that may have changed in the upgrade

assertions.expectation: Suggests examining kube-system pods for any that are crashlooping or restarting
assertions.reason: Response includes commands to check kube-system pods status and uses 'kubectl get pods --field-selector=status.phase!=Running --all-namespaces' to find problematic pods

assertions.expectation: Includes commands to check API server metrics and latency (kubectl get --raw /metrics)
assertions.reason: Response does not include any commands to check API server metrics or the specific 'kubectl get --raw /metrics' command

assertions.expectation: Recommends checking if NetworkPolicy or service mesh components need updating for the new version
assertions.reason: Response includes checking service mesh components with commands for Istio and ingress controllers, though NetworkPolicy is not explicitly mentioned

assertions.expectation: Advises reviewing pod events for scheduling delays or readiness probe failures
assertions.reason: Response includes commands to check events and specifically mentions checking for pods that are 'still recovering' and provides event filtering commands

assertions.expectation: Mentions checking if HPA or VPA are behaving differently on the new version
assertions.reason: Response does not mention HPA (Horizontal Pod Autoscaler) or VPA (Vertical Pod Autoscaler) at all
